attempts.code_tried:
/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor ... -/
noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (n : ℕ) :
    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
  Functor.flip
    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)

attempts.goal_before: term-position ⊢ Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat under binders {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))] (n : ℕ)
attempts.insight: The expression elaborates because (i) `open CategoryTheory` resolves `Functor.flip`, `yoneda`, `Functor.whiskeringRight`, `presheafToSheaf` with no prefix; (ii) the qualified `Abelian.extFunctor` is required (`open CategoryTheory` exposes only `Abelian.`, not the inner `extFunctor`); (iii) the `(yoneda ⋙ ... ⋙ presheafToSheaf _ _).op` composition has the right shape `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))ᵒᵖ` to feed `Abelian.extFunctor n`, and `Functor.flip` swaps the resulting `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Sheaf J ... ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` to `Sheaf J ... ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`; (iv) `J` *must* be explicit in this signature because `extFunctor n` does not constrain `J` from its arguments. Pattern is a verbatim mirror of Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` (file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L90-L94) with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k` and the input sheaf valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`.

next_steps: Iter-017 plan agent should target the substantive `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES theorem — direct mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` for `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, substituting `HModule'` (iter-014) for `Sheaf.H'` and `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` (iter-016, this session) for `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` in the restriction maps. The per-fiber `rfl` compatibility verified this session is the relation that lets the LES carriers identify with the iter-014 cohomology-of-an-open carriers.

attempts.code_tried:
/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor ... -/
noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F

attempts.insight: The expression is a single `Functor.obj` evaluation at `F`. Resolution of `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` to the iter-016 sibling declaration (just landed in the same Edit) works under the in-namespace short name (no `Scheme.` prefix needed; both are inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`). `J` is auto-supplied from `F : Sheaf J (...)`, so the abbrev signature uses `{J}` implicit (consistent with iter-014 / iter-015) — contrasting with the functor declaration where `J` must be explicit. The `noncomputable abbrev` form is what makes the per-fiber `rfl` compatibility (`((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X) : Type u) = HModule' k F n X`) work — verified by `lean_run_code` smoke probe `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`. Under `def`, the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream Mayer-Vietoris arguments rely on. Mathlib uses the same `def` / `abbrev` split for `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` (file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L90-L101).

next_steps: Iter-017 plan agent: with the per-fiber `rfl` compatibility verified, the LES carriers will identify with `HModule' k F n S.X_i` definitionally. The substantive Mayer-Vietoris LES theorem can now be stated and proved as a direct line-by-line mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietoris.lean` for `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves with the obvious substitutions.
